articles.title: Layered Light in a Milan Heritage Hotel Lobby
articles.summary: How a Milanese heritage hotel transformed its lobby into a flexible day-to-night social hub with layered lighting and modular seating.
articles.content: The renovation of this 1920s palazzo in central Milan focused on turning a traditional check‑in hall into a multi‑use lobby lounge. Designers introduced a hierarchy of lighting—from concealed cove lighting to focused task lamps—to support co‑working, casual meetings, and evening aperitivo service. A muted stone palette is animated with brass detailing and custom rugs zoned for different activities. The check‑in desks have been replaced with a concierge island and roaming hosts equipped with tablets, freeing the perimeter walls for art and soft seating.

articles.title: Art Deco Lobby Reboot at a Paris Boutique Hotel
articles.summary: A compact Paris lobby channels cinematic Art Deco references to create a memorable arrival sequence within 80 square meters.
articles.content: The design team leaned into bold geometry and reflective finishes to exaggerate the perceived scale of this small lobby. Mirrored columns, fluted glass, and a deep teal palette are balanced by warm walnut joinery and integrated banquettes. A custom terrazzo floor guides guests from the entrance to reception, while sculptural pendants anchor the seating zones. The lobby doubles as an intimate cocktail bar after dark, thanks to concealed bottle storage and adaptable lighting scenes.

articles.title: Biophilic Lobby Concepts for a Lisbon Hilltop Hotel
articles.summary: Terraced planting, daylight, and tactile materials define a new biophilic lobby in Lisbon with views over the Tagus River.
articles.content: Responding to the hotel’s hilltop location, the lobby is organized as a series of terraces stepping down towards panoramic windows. Planters double as space dividers, filled with Mediterranean species that thrive in the bright exposure. A restrained palette of limewashed walls, locally quarried stone, and woven cane references vernacular architecture. The lobby’s circulation integrates informal seating, encouraging guests to pause rather than pass through.

articles.title: Lobby Lounge Hybrids Redefine Guest Arrival in Berlin
articles.summary: Berlin hotels experiment with lobby lounges that blur the line between reception, co‑working, and nightlife.
articles.content: From Mitte to Kreuzberg, Berlin’s newest hotels are rethinking the lobby as an urban living room. This roundup surveys four properties that integrate co‑working tables, DJ booths, and micro‑galleries directly into the arrival space. The common thread: lobbies that prioritize resident‑style comfort over formality.

articles.title: Slow Lobby Design for a Copenhagen Waterfront Hotel
articles.summary: Nordic materiality and a slowed‑down arrival sequence frame the guest experience in this Copenhagen lobby.
articles.content: Inspired by the harbour’s tidal rhythm, the lobby’s design stacks low timber platforms and generous stair landings to choreograph a slower arrival. Guests move past a coffee bar, gallery corner, and reading lounge before reaching reception. Natural wool textiles, oak, and locally made ceramics amplify the tactile experience.

articles.title: Lobby as Urban Playground in a New York Lifestyle Hotel
articles.summary: A New York lifestyle hotel uses its lobby to connect guests with local makers, DJs, and pop‑up retail.
articles.content: The ground floor is conceived as a flexible event platform with rolling display units, plug‑and‑play DJ stations, and a central bar that anchors the space.

articles.title: Top 10 Hotel Lobby Lighting Ideas for 2024
articles.summary: A 2024 roundup of hotel lobby lighting strategies, from sculptural pendants to integrated handrail LEDs.
articles.content: This Top 10 roundup gathers global examples of hotel lobby lighting that balance drama, wayfinding, and energy efficiency. From dynamic media ceilings in Seoul to softly glowing alabaster columns in Madrid, each project demonstrates how lighting can articulate zoning and support changing guest behaviors. The article breaks down fixtures, control systems, and color temperatures used in each case study, with tips for specifying in your own hotel projects.

articles.title: Top 10 Beach Resort Lighting Moves in 2024
articles.summary: From pool decks to spa corridors, these 10 resort projects show how lighting shapes after‑dark experiences.
articles.content: Resort designers are using layered lighting and programmable scenes to respond to changing guest rhythms across the day.

articles.title: How Lighting Carries Brand Story in Boutique Hotels
articles.summary: Designers share how they use lighting to translate brand values into spatial experiences across guestrooms and lobbies.
articles.content: Through interviews with three studios, this article unpacks the role of lighting in communicating narrative and brand DNA.

articles.title: Cliffside Resort Lobby Frames the Bali Sunrise
articles.summary: A stepped lobby amphitheater in Bali acts as a sunrise viewing platform and event space.
articles.content: The lobby’s tiered seating and operable glazing blur boundaries between interior and landscape.

articles.title: 2025 Resort Poolside Lounge Chairs: Product Roundup
articles.summary: A curated selection of 15 lounge chairs for resort pools, from modular platforms to ultra‑slim loungers.
articles.content: This product roundup compares dimensions, materials, and lead times for poolside lounge options suitable for resorts in humid climates.

articles.title: North American Office Lobbies Embrace Wellness Signals
articles.summary: As workers return to the office, lobby spaces are retooled with wellness‑driven materials and programs.
articles.content: This case study roundup contrasts three office lobbies that prioritize air quality, daylight, and biophilia.

articles.title: Coworking Spaces Look More Like Boutique Hotels
articles.summary: The latest coworking interiors borrow heavily from hospitality, from concierge desks to residential kitchens.
articles.content: Operators are betting on hospitality‑grade design to differentiate flexible workspaces.

articles.title: Low‑Carbon Hospitality: Inside a Timber Hotel in Quebec
articles.summary: Mass timber, local sourcing, and smart systems reduce operational and embodied carbon in this lakeside hotel.
articles.content: A deep dive into structure, envelope, and MEP decisions that lower the project’s carbon footprint.

articles.title: Lifestyle Hotel Brand Acquires Heritage Properties in Spain
articles.summary: The brand plans to reposition five Spanish heritage buildings as design‑led hotels by 2028.
articles.content: We examine the development pipeline and design opportunities across the portfolio.

articles.title: Opinion: Why the Hotel Lobby Is Still the Brand Heart
articles.summary: Amid mobile check‑in and keyless entry, the lobby remains the emotional core of the hotel.
articles.content: An opinion piece arguing for continued investment in lobby design as a primary brand expression.

articles.title: High‑Contrast Cocktail Bar Layers Shadow and Neon in Tokyo
articles.summary: A compact bar in Shibuya uses light and shadow to frame intimate seating pockets.
articles.content: This project profile unpacks the material palette and lighting strategy for a 55‑seat bar in Tokyo.

articles.title: Designer Interview: Aya Tanaka on Quiet Luxury in Japan’s Ryokans
articles.summary: Tokyo‑based interior designer Aya Tanaka discusses her approach to lighting and materiality in contemporary ryokan projects.
articles.content: In this designer interview, Tanaka reflects on translating traditional Japanese hospitality into modern boutique ryokans.

events.title: Lighting and Layout for High‑Turnover Restaurants
events.description: A live webinar for hospitality designers focused on restaurant layouts, bar adjacencies, and layered lighting strategies to boost covers without sacrificing experience.

events.title: Menu Engineering Meets Interior Design for Restaurants
events.description: This October webinar pairs an F&B strategist with an interior designer to discuss how menu structure and physical layout work together.

events.title: Hotel Design Futures Conference 2025
events.description: A two‑day conference in New York covering hotel lobbies, guestrooms, and sustainability strategies.

events.title: Crafting Intimate Bar Interiors
events.description: An in‑person workshop for designers focused on acoustics, lighting, and seating layouts in small bars.

events.title: Asia‑Pacific Resort Design Expo 2026
events.description: A trade show featuring resort case studies, products, and supplier showcases across the Asia‑Pacific region.

events.title: Hybrid Office & Coworking Meetup Berlin
events.description: An informal meetup for designers exploring the crossover between office and hospitality design.

events.title: Specifying Hotel Lighting Packages in 2024
events.description: A webinar focused on coordinating decorative and architectural lighting packages for hotel projects.

events.title: Sustainable Hospitality Interiors Awards 2025
events.description: An international awards program recognizing low‑carbon and circular design strategies in hospitality.

events.title: Coworking & Hospitality Convergence Summit
events.description: A regional conference on the convergence of coworking and hospitality in the Asia‑Pacific region.

events.title: Designing Soundscapes for Bars & Lounges
events.description: An online session exploring acoustic treatments and AV strategies for bar projects.

profiles.name: Aya Tanaka
profiles.biography: Designer interview with Tokyo‑based interior designer Aya Tanaka, whose studio focuses on contemporary ryokan and boutique hotel projects across Japan. Tanaka blends quiet luxury with rigorous detailing, often collaborating closely with lighting designers and artisans.

profiles.name: Kenji Mori
profiles.biography: Kenji Mori leads hospitality and F&B projects across Japan, from izakaya bars to urban business hotels.

profiles.name: Studio North
profiles.biography: Studio North is a Vancouver‑based practice specializing in hotel lobbies, mountain resorts, and spa interiors.

profiles.name: Lucia Bianchi
profiles.biography: Lucia Bianchi leads lobby and public area concepts for international hotel brands, with a focus on flexible community‑driven ground floors.

profiles.name: Omar Hassan
profiles.biography: Omar advises hotel and resort brands on low‑carbon retrofits and ESG reporting.

profiles.name: Lumen Lab
profiles.biography: Lumen Lab collaborates with interior designers on hotel, bar, and coworking projects across Europe, focusing on human‑centric lighting.

profiles.name: Sofia Martins
profiles.biography: Sofia leads resort and beachfront hospitality projects throughout Brazil.

profiles.name: Maya Chen
profiles.biography: Maya works at the intersection of digital and physical experience for hotels and serviced apartments.

profiles.name: Carlos Rodriguez
profiles.biography: Carlos specializes in immersive bar and speakeasy concepts in Europe and Latin America.

profiles.name: Hikari Design Office
profiles.biography: Hikari Design Office develops small hotels, cafés, and coworking spaces across Kansai.

projects.title: Neon Lantern Rooftop Bar
projects.summary: A 120‑seat rooftop bar with layered neon and city‑view terraces in Lisbon.
projects.description: Perched above Lisbon’s historic center, Neon Lantern uses a palette of dark terrazzo, mirrored ceilings, and programmable neon arcs to create an immersive cocktail bar experience. Seating ranges from intimate booths to communal high‑tops, with a central bar anchoring the plan.

projects.title: Copper Rail Cocktail Bar
projects.summary: An 80‑seat cocktail bar tucked beneath a heritage train station in Chicago.
projects.description: Borrowing cues from classic railcars, Copper Rail layers warm timber, ribbed glass, and a signature copper footrail along the bar. Acoustic treatment and intimate booth lighting support a moody, late‑night atmosphere.

projects.title: Skyline Atrium Lobby, Chicago
projects.summary: An 850 sqm lobby with a soaring atrium, co‑working terraces, and a feature stair overlooking the Chicago River.
projects.description: The Skyline Atrium Lobby repositions a downtown business hotel as a lifestyle destination. The double‑height volume is carved into terraces with a central sculptural stair, connecting reception, co‑working, and bar areas. Joinery and lighting subtly guide circulation while maintaining visual openness.

projects.title: Canyon Vista Lobby, Phoenix
projects.summary: A desert‑inspired 620 sqm lobby for a resort‑style urban hotel in Phoenix.
projects.description: Canyon Vista Lobby uses stratified stone, terracotta hues, and filtered daylight to evoke the nearby canyon landscape. Seating is organized as a series of landforms, with integrated planting and locally commissioned art.

projects.title: Harborfront Grand Lobby, Vancouver
projects.summary: A 940 sqm waterfront lobby that doubles as an events venue overlooking the harbor.
projects.description: This large lobby features a flexible central forum space flanked by check‑in pods, lounge seating, and a café. Operable partitions allow the space to host conferences and community events.

projects.title: Montreal Courtyard Lobby
projects.summary: A 520 sqm courtyard‑focused lobby for a boutique hotel in Old Montreal.
projects.description: The lobby wraps a glazed courtyard, creating a sequence of intimate lounge pockets and a daylit reception zone.

projects.title: Lantern Lane Micro Bar, Tokyo
projects.summary: A 28‑seat micro bar in Tokyo’s Shinjuku district with layered lantern lighting.
projects.description: Compact fixed seating, standing ledges, and layered shoji‑inspired lanterns define this micro bar, designed for quick visits and intimate gatherings.

projects.title: Seaside Cascade Lobby, Bali
projects.summary: A stepped lobby amphitheater at a cliffside resort in Bali.
projects.description: Guests arrive via a descending sequence of terraces that frame views of the ocean, with integrated pools and planted edges softening the architecture.

projects.title: Coral Strand Resort & Villas
projects.summary: A 5‑star beach resort in Phuket with lagoon pools and villa clusters.
projects.description: Coral Strand brings a layered guest journey from arrival court to beachfront, with a porous lobby linking indoor lounges and outdoor terraces.

projects.title: WerkHaus Cowork Lobby, Berlin
projects.summary: A hybrid lobby and coworking hub in a Berlin hotel‑office building.
projects.description: The project merges reception, café, and dedicated coworking zones within a former bank hall.

projects.title: Shinjuku Transit Hotel Lobby
projects.summary: A compact lobby for a business hotel above a Tokyo transit hub.
projects.description: Designed around speed and clarity, the lobby uses bold wayfinding, clear sightlines, and efficient seating clusters.

projects.title: Highline Brasserie & Bar, Denver
projects.summary: A brasserie and bar adjacent to a hotel lobby in downtown Denver.
projects.description: Highline blurs the threshold between hotel lobby and street‑facing brasserie with sliding glass walls and shared seating.

projects.title: Sky Orchard Rooftop Bar
projects.summary: A lush rooftop bar with tropical planting atop a Singapore hotel.
projects.description: Sky Orchard uses dense planting, reflective pools, and integrated banquette seating to create a resort‑like bar in the city.

projects.title: Harbor Tower Office Lobby
projects.summary: A workplace lobby in Toronto reimagined with hospitality cues.
projects.description: Soft seating, a public café, and gallery wall bring hospitality warmth into a corporate tower lobby.

projects.title: Casa CoLab Coworking Lobby, Mexico City
projects.summary: A coworking lobby in a converted residence in Mexico City, blending domestic and hospitality cues.
projects.description: Casa CoLab’s lobby acts as a shared living room for members and guests, with a café, soft seating, and flexible event space.
